headline: Ph.D. Research Scientist | Cardiovascular Biology | Preclinical Research | Translational Medicine
education: Ph.D. Molecular Genetics, University of Delhi (UGC-NET JRF AIR 64; ICMR JRF Rank 15)
custom_question_context: Ph.D. Molecular Genetics from University of Delhi (UGC-NET JRF AIR 64; ICMR JRF Rank 15) with 10+ years of preclinical research. Co-first author of a Nature Communications 2026 paper (5,093 accesses, Altmetric 78) validating PTRH2 as a therapeutic target in peripartum cardiomyopathy — directly translatable to therapeutic strategy. Currently Post-Doctoral Research Scientist at The Lundquist Institute / Harbor-UCLA Medical Center, Torrance, CA. In vivo expertise: established PTRH2-KO pregnant mouse model; complete cardiac phenotyping pipeline (Langendorff isolation, contractility assays, VEVO F2 echocardiography, EKG); managed 200+ mouse colony across 3 transgenic lines. Assay expertise: Beta-gal, TUNEL, FACS, Western blot, IHC/ICC, ELISA, qRT-PCR, XTT/MTT, Hydroxyproline. Multi-omics: bulk RNA-seq, scRNA-seq, Xenium & Visium spatial transcriptomics; IPA pathway analysis; STRING network analysis. Grant support: contributed to CIRM grant, Cohen Fellowship, PCVRD clinical trial workplan. Mentored 4-6 graduate and undergraduate scientists. Active peer reviewer (12 reviews in 2025). Seeking industry transition to R&D scientist, preclinical research, or biomarker discovery role at biotech/pharma/CRO. Open to on-site roles anywhere in the US or internationally.
